entry.title: Streamlining SAP CPI Migration: A Postman-Driven Guide from Neo to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
CPI Migration: A Postman-Driven Guide from Neo to Cloud Foundry

Introduction

Migrating from the Neo environment to Cloud Foundry (CF) is not just a technical necessity. It is also a chance to review and improve your integration landscape. While SAP provides the Migration Tooling, using Postman collections with the OData API gives you a detailed, repeatable, and clear method to transfer your artifacts. Using Postman collections with CPI APIs can automate the migration process and boost productivity.

Pre-requisite: 1. Identify the Correct Service Plan

Make sure after creating the sub account, respective service plan has to be created in the Integration suite.

2. Create a Service Instance

Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions and create a new instance of the Process Integration Runtime.

Service: Process Integration Runtime Plan: api, integration-flow Runtime Environment: Cloud Foundry

3. Assign Necessary Roles (JSON Configuration)

During instance creation, you need to define the authorities. Use a JSON structure to grant the roles needed for migration, such as: 1. WorkspacePackagesRead or WorkspacePackagesWrite 2. KeyStoreRead or KeyStoreWrite 3. AuthGroup_IntegrationDeveloper 4. AuthGroup.Administrator

4. Generate the Service Key

After INSTANCE is created, click the Create service key. This will generate a JSON block containing the:

l ClientId

l ClientSecret

l Token URL

Migration Steps Using Postman

 

CPI MIG010 Connect to Tenants

CPI MIG020 Readiness Checks

CPI MIG030 Variables

CPI MIG050 Custom Tags

CPI MIG060 Custom Public Certificates

CPI MIG070 User Credentials

CPI MIG080 OAuth2 Client Credentials

CPI MIG110 Pre-Packaged Content

CPI MIG120 Custom Content

CPI MIG130 Value Mapping Values

 

 

 

Step-by-Step Migration Workflow

 

 

1. Establishing the Foundation (MIG010 - MIG030)

Before migrating, you must establish a secure handshake between your environments. CPI MIG010 Connect to Tenants: This is your starting point. You'll set up your Environment Variables in Postman to store the Host URLs and OAuth tokens for both Neo (Source) and CF (Target).

 

 

CPI MIG020 Readiness Checks: This step uses APIs to check if the target Cloud Foundry tenant is active and has the required service plans, specifically Process Integration Runtime, assigned.

 

CPI MIG030 Variables: Many iflows use Write Variables. This collection makes sure that persistent data stored in the Neo database is captured and ready to be recreated in the CF tenant.

 

2. Migrating Security and Identity (MIG050 - MIG080)

Security artifacts are often the overlooked elements that lead to migration failures during testing.

CPI MIG050 Custom Tags: Transfer your metadata. Custom tags help you organize and search for iFlows. Keeping these consistent ensures your governance model remains intact.

CPI MIG060 Custom Public Certificates: Move your X.509 certificates. Make sure to export the public keys from Neo and post them to the CF Keystore. This helps maintain trust with external partners.

CPI MIG070 and MIG080 Credentials: Whether you are using User Credentials (Basic Auth) or OAuth2 Client Credentials, these steps automate the creation of security material entries. 3. Content Transfer (MIG110, MIG130)

This is the key part of the migration: moving the integration logic.

CPI MIG110 Pre-Packaged Content: For standard SAP content, this ensures you are subscribed to the latest versions in the CF Business Accelerator Hub.

CPI MIG120 Custom Content: This is the main phase. This collection transfers the .zip bundles of your custom iFlows from Neo to the CF Design workspace.

CPI MIG130 Value Mapping: Value mappings often have thousands of entries. Using the API to migrate these is much faster and less prone to errors than manual exports.

Post-Migration activity :

Monitor : Monitor the integration suite closely all the iflows for more than 1 week.

Clear the Cache: After migrating certificates (MIG060), give the CF runtime a few minutes to refresh the keystore.

 

Conclusion : If you're doing a Neo migration over to Cloud Foundry, using Postman collections could really change things up. It turns that whole manual setup, which is full of mistakes and takes forever, into something more organized. You know, repeatable steps that you can just run again without hassle, and even automate parts of it. That seems helpful for keeping the Integration Suite clean, I guess. No mess left behind.

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Overview

If you are integrating your Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone, you may face

the challenge of exposing derived and composite roles containing launchpad content.

Creating (PFCG) roles on ABAP system, that derive from roles with launchpad content, is mainly driven by the need to differentiate authorization for the same set of applications. This often leads to many roles deriving from the same base role. In such cases, the derived roles are assigned to users instead of their base roles. The derived roles get the content of the role menu, like Fiori catalogs or spaces from inheritance and focus mainly on authorizations and user assignments.

This blog outlines the approach for exposing derived roles from an ABAP system to SAP Build Work Zone.

Related Blog

In the blog How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone you will learn two options when exposing composite roles. The recommended approach for integrating derived roles is the same as outlined in “Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)” when exposing composite roles.

Integrating Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone

When exposing derived roles to SAP Build Work Zone there is a separation of concerns between the exposure of launchpad content, authorization and role-to-user assignment.

For the exposure, you select only the single roles that contain launchpad content in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE. Derived roles are not supported in the exposure report. This restriction prevents exposing a high number of roles with identical launchpad content. When the content for exposure is processed, the system identifies roles which derive from the selected roles and includes these references in the exposed CDM content (as relatedRoleAssignments). These references are not exposed to the administrator during exposure but used by the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) and SAP Build Work Zone infrastructure.

 

Identity Provisioning Service and SAP Build Work Zone handle the role assignment logic, ensuring that users with derived roles on the ABAP side get the correct roles assigned on the BTP side. With that, users get access to the application they are authorized for.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about roles deriving from it and their relationships, but those derived roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The IPS ensures that when an user is assigned to a derived role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information, see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • This approach is supported since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022 and later.

 

Conclusion 

Integrating derived roles with SAP Build Work Zone works best with IPS-driven provisioning, as it preserves existing role structure and automated user to role assignment. Without IPS one must manually ensure that corresponding roles in both BTP and ABAP system are assigned, which is impractical in larger scenarios. The IPS based approach also supports the integration of composite roles, which are often used together with derived roles.  In that case only single roles should be exposed. Information about related derived or composite roles is provided though CDM content and processed by SAP Build Work Zone and IPS.

 

Further Information

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Restrictions

entry.title: Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Fiori Elements provides several floorplans that help developers build enterprise applications quickly with minimal coding. One of these floorplans is the Overview Page (OVP). The Overview Page acts as a role-based dashboard that displays important business information using visual and interactive cards. It enables users to monitor key performance indicators, review important data, and take actions directly from a single screen.

Overview Page in Fiori Elements: The Overview Page (OVP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a role-based dashboard that displays important business information using cards.

Overview Page  is Dashboard of multiple cards showing business insights.

Note:  In this article, I have not covered the step-by-step process of creating the CDS View and OData Service. I have focused mainly on the key steps required to configure and implement the Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements.

CDS View:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZOVR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'OverView Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true




define view Ztest_Overview_Report
  as select from Ztest_ALP_Report
{


      .selectionField: [{position: 10  }]
      @EndUserText.label: 'Month & Year'
  key Month_Year,
      @EndUserText.label: 'Total Amount'
      max(Amount) as Total_Amount


}

group by
  Month_Year

Consume CDS View in OData Service:

Overview Page (OVP) - BAS:

I have covered only the Analytical Card in the Overview Page. The remaining card types such as List Card, Table Card, KPI Card, and Link List Card are not included in this blog.

Please follow the steps below to create and configure the Analytical Card step by step in the SAP Fiori Elements Overview Page in BTP BAS.

 

 

Step1:

    <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_Overview_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Donut"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Sales Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Total_Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Total_Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>

Step2:

 <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="step2_PVQ">
                    <Record>
                        <PropertyValue Property="SortOrder">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.SortOrderType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Property" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Descending" Bool="true"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step3:

 

 <Annotation Term="UI.DataPoint" Qualifier="Step3_DPQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.DataPointType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Month_Year"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Criticality" EnumMember="UI.CriticalityType/Information"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Total Amount"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Total Amount"/>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step4:

<Annotation Term="UI.Identification" Qualifier="Step4_Q">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="Total_Amount"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Total Amount"/>
                            <Annotation Term="UI.Importance" EnumMember="UI.ImportanceType/Medium"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>

Step5:

 

"cards": {
        "Nayak333": {
            "model": "mainModel",
            "template": "sap.ovp.cards.charts.analytical",
            "settings": {
                "title": "Analytical Card",
                "entitySet": "Ztest_Overview_Report",
                "dataPointAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.DataPoint#Step3_DPQ",
                "valueSelectionInfo": "Total_Amount",
                "presentationAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.PresentationVariant#step2_PVQ",
                "chartAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.Chart#Step_CQ",
                "identificationAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.Identification#Step4_Q"
            }
        }
    },

 

Full Annotation Code:

<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_Overview_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Donut"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Sales Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Total_Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Total_Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="step2_PVQ">
                    <Record>
                        <PropertyValue Property="SortOrder">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.SortOrderType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Property" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Descending" Bool="true"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.DataPoint" Qualifier="Step3_DPQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.DataPointType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Month_Year"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Criticality" EnumMember="UI.CriticalityType/Information"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Total Amount"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Total Amount"/>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.Identification" Qualifier="Step4_Q">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="Total_Amount"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Total Amount"/>
                            <Annotation Term="UI.Importance" EnumMember="UI.ImportanceType/Medium"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

Note:   Please follow the same steps to create the remaining card types in the Overview Page. By configuring them in a similar way, the other cards will also be displayed on the Overview Page.

 

Conclusion

The Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides a powerful way to display business insights through a dashboard of visual cards. It helps users quickly monitor key metrics, identify important tasks, and navigate to detailed applications for further analysis. By combining data visualization and actionable insights, the Overview Page enables organizations to make faster and more informed decisions.

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Thank you all for your feedback. You can be for sure that this is heard and considered going forward. We are working hard to provide you an approach within the LCNC area at SAP Build towards the application development. The evolution and great progress in AI especially in the app development area required a shift in our product direction to push our AI development forward and provide you the most easy and productive way to create applications. SAP SAPPHIRE is usually an SAP event in which product updates and new products are introduced.
I'll keep you posted. Please don't hesitate to provide further feedback.

entry.title: Custom Business Configuration Sorting the Table using Non-KeyFields
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently using the Custom Business Configuration and have generated some entities. I am currently having issue sorting the table, I have tried using annotations like the one below in the Metadata Extension.


Seems like even with this, it is getting ignored and still sorts by key fields.

Scenario:

Operation - KeyfieldCounter - KeyfieldCountry - Non-Keyfield
Flight1V
Flight2A
Flight3D

I want to be able to sort the table by default when opening the application by country which is not a key field and ignore the keyfields with the expected output:

Operation - KeyfieldCounter - KeyfieldCountry - Non - Keyfield
Flight2A
Flight3D
Flight1V

In this case, the keyfields was set to hidden for user and will only see the country column, so users would want it to be sorted.

Does anyone have any work around on this?

entry.title: Re: Rethink ALM: Moving to SAP Cloud ALM as a Clean Core Strategy for Application Lifecycle Manageme
entry.content: Excellent perspective on clean core as an ALM strategy. One challenge I've seen in practice is that while Cloud ALM handles requirements and basic deployment orchestration well, governance gaps emerge when BTP extensions and core SAP transports need to move in sync  the complexity doesn't disappear, it just shifts to the application perimeter. Iconiq  Has anyone found a reliable pattern for unified governance across S/4HANA and BTP in Cloud ALM without pulling in third-party tools?

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Context Attributes for SAP Released Event Types
entry.content: This is exactly the pattern we've been looking for in event-driven architecture with SAP. The use case of filtering Sales Order events based on sales organization or document type at the header level  rather than consuming the full payload and filtering in the application layer  makes a significant difference in integration performance at scale. One follow-up question: is there a limit to how many custom context attributes can be promoted per event type, and does that limit vary between S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Private Cloud?

entry.title: Available quantity in bin is zero, even though i have enough stock in Bin
entry.content:
Text of question in warehouse task creation log:

***** Product WT Creation *****
For warehouse request PDO 4922 10
For product EWMS4-02; quantity 15.00000000000000 PC
***** Determine source data - start *****
Determining source data with storage type:, section:, bin:, HU:
Storage type search with full keys: No entry found
Storage type search with wildcard: Sequence no. 3 used successfully
Storage type search with wildcard: Search sequence REPL, rule FIFO found
Determine stock in source storage type 0050
Determine stock in source storage bin REPL.005
Available quantity in bin is zero
Determine stock in source storage bin REPL.01.01
Available quantity in bin is zero
Determine stock in source storage type Y011
Determine stock in source storage type Y021
System could not determine source storage bin
No warehouse task created

I do add enough stock in REPL.01.01, why system can not find it?

entry.title: Upcoming cloud system downtimes
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We currently receive individual email notifications for planned downtimes across our SAP cloud systems. However, these come separately per system, making it difficult to plan and manage our maintenance windows effectively.

Request: We would like to know if there is a single consolidated report or dashboard that lists all upcoming scheduled downtimes across all our cloud systems in one place — rather than tracking them individually through separate email notifications.

Please advise on the best approach to achieve this.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming cloud system downtimes
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know there is a Planned Events for Cloud Service application in Calendar dashboard in SAP for Me. Refer to KBA 3106407 - How to set up and use Calendar - SAP for Me.

Could you pls kindly check and see if you can access this app to have a general overview about all the upcoming system downtimes?

You may also access the Availability application in SAP for Me to check all the events for the cloud services. Refer to KBA 2548400 - Getting started with the SAP Cloud Availability - SAP for Me.

Hope this could be of any help here. Any other issue, feel free to contact us again.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Connection to IXOS Server from CPI - Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi @PavanKalyan_Racha96 ,

Use the IXOS ArchiveLink HTTP interface to deal directly with the OpenText Archive Server.  Whether you receive a unique docId, or you generate one yourself and force it upon Archive Server, is up to you.

If you prefer asynchronous batch processing, then Document Pipelines should be looked into.  Again, you can either decide to generate your own docId values, or have IXOS / OpenText provide them for you.

entry.title: Re: Data Replication Proxy - Time Off
entry.content:
Hi Cindy,

When you select yes it will create only the missing entries. For whatever reason it recreates old entries again payroll should not have any impact as the data is same. 

we have used many times without any issue. 

regards

Sanjay

entry.title: How We Built an Efficient Contingent Data Management Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Introduction

In many organizations using SAP SuccessFactors HCM, contingent workers play a critical role in supporting day-to-day operations and project-based work. But managing this workforce isn’t always as simple as it sounds.

From what I’ve seen, things start to get complicated when the number of contingent workers begins to grow.

HR teams often find themselves handling updates manually — whether it’s hiring new contingent workers, extending contracts, processing transfers, or updating work orders. Each action might seem small on its own, but when multiplied across dozens or even hundreds of workers, it quickly becomes time-consuming and difficult to manage.

I’ve noticed that a lot of this work still depends heavily on spreadsheets, repeated data entry, and constant back-and-forth validations. Contract extensions get delayed, transfers take longer than expected, and even a small mistake in updating a record can lead to inconsistencies in the system.

What really stood out to me was how reactive the process becomes. Instead of having a structured way to manage contingent workforce data, teams are often rushing to keep everything up to date — especially during peak periods or project ramp-ups.

At the same time, scaling this process isn’t easy. As organizations rely more on flexible workforce models, the need to manage large volumes of contingent workers increases. But without the right tools, it becomes harder to maintain speed, accuracy, and control.

That’s when it became clear that managing contingent workforce data needed a smarter, more centralized approach — one that reduces manual effort while still maintaining governance and data integrity.

Our Approach

To address these challenges, we built the Contingent Data Management Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

While working on this solution, my goal was very clear: simplify complex operations without losing control. I wanted to create something that reduces the dependency on manual effort while still ensuring that every action is accurate, traceable, and compliant.

The application provides a structured and intuitive interface where HR teams and administrators can manage contingent workforce operations more efficiently. Instead of updating records one by one or relying on external files, users can perform mass actions such as hiring, importing, updating, extending contracts, and transferring contingent workers — all within a controlled environment.

What I focused on most was making sure the solution could handle scale. Whether it’s a small batch of updates or a large volume of contingent workers, the system is designed to process changes quickly while maintaining consistency across all records.

At the same time, automation doesn’t mean compromising on governance. Every action still follows the necessary validations, business rules, and audit requirements. The difference is that HR teams no longer have to do the repetitive heavy lifting — the system takes care of it.

With this approach, organizations can manage their contingent workforce with far more efficiency and confidence. What used to be a manual and error-prone process becomes streamlined, scalable, and much easier to control.

Key Features and Benefits

1. User- Friendly Interface

  • SAP Fiori-based responsive UI.
  • Guided steps for data entry, validation, and submission.
  • Supports desktop and mobile access via SAP Build Work Zone.

2. Mass Contingent Worker Hiring and Rehire Management

  • Create and process multiple hires or rehires in a single operation.
  • Preserves and significantly reduces hiring cycle time for mass recruitment.
  • Minimizes manual intervention and repetitive HR tasks.
  • Ensures contingent worker data is validated and created consistently in SAP SuccessFactors.

3. Download Standard Hiring Template

  • Provides a predefined, SuccessFactors-aligned template for mass hiring data entry.
  • Ensures users follow the correct format, mandatory fields, and data structure.
  • Reduces upload errors caused by incorrect column names or data formats.
  • Can be easily shared with recruitment partners or external agencies.

4. Import Data

  • Allows HR users to import data using structured files (CSV / JSON / XML).
  • Eliminates manual data entry for large hiring volumes.
  • Also allows HR users to manually create a single record directly from the application UI.
  • Supports validation of uploaded data before submission to SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Handles errors gracefully by highlighting invalid or missing fields.

5. Data Validation with Error Highlighting and Logs

  • Performs real-time and pre-processing validation on imported hiring data before submission to SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Validates mandatory fields, data formats, and business rules aligned with SuccessFactors requirements.
  • Highlights validation errors clearly at row and field level, making it easy for users to identify and correct issues.
  • Prevents partial or incorrect data from being processed, ensuring data integrity.

6. Download Validation Logs

  • Provides the ability to download detailed error logs for failed records.
  • Error logs include clear reasons for failure (e.g., missing mandatory fields, invalid values).
  • Enables quick correction and re-upload of only failed records.

7. Download Successfully Imported Records

  • Successfully imported records can be downloadable.
  • Users can download the list of successfully imported records for confirmation, and audit.
  • Provides full transparency on which records were created successfully in SAP SuccessFactors.

8. Mass Update Contract

  • Enables HR users to extend or terminate contracts for one or multiple contingent workers in a single operation.
  • Allows administrators to select the contingent workers whose contracts need to be updated directly from the application interface.
  • Supports updating contract end dates to extend assignments or terminating contracts when engagements end.

9. Mass Edit Work Order

  • Allows HR administrators to modify work order information for contingent workers directly from the application.
  • Provides a structured and controlled way to maintain accurate work order data without navigating through multiple SuccessFactors screens.
  • Minimizes manual intervention and repetitive HR tasks.
  • Enables users to apply updates to one or multiple contingent worker records simultaneously, improving operational efficiency.
  • Helps organizations quickly adjust workforce assignments and operational details as business needs change.

10. Mass Transfer Contract

  • Enables managers to transfer contingent workers who report to them to another manager directly from the application.
  • Allows the logged-in manager to select one or multiple contingent workers and initiate a manager transfer in a single action.
  • Supports updating reporting relationships efficiently when organizational or project changes occur.
  • Helps organizations quickly realign contingent workforce reporting structures without manual updates in multiple systems.
  • Minimizes manual intervention and repetitive HR tasks.
  • Ensures contingent worker data is validated and created consistently in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Security & Access Control

  • The application enforces role-based access control (RBAC) to ensure users can only access data relevant to their roles and responsibilities.
  • Managers can view and manage only the contingent workers who directly report to them, ensuring controlled visibility of workforce data.
  • Access to key operations such as contract extensions, work order updates, and manager transfers is governed through role-based permissions.
  • The solution helps maintain data security and organizational compliance by restricting unauthorized access to contingent worker information.
  • Integration with SAP SuccessFactors APIs ensures secure and reliable data synchronization between systems.

Business Value

  • The solution enables organizations to efficiently manage contingent workforce operations at scale by supporting mass activities such as hiring, contract extensions, work order updates, and manager transfers.
  • Built-in validations ensure that only complete and consistent data is processed, reducing errors in contingent worker records and ensuring alignment with SAP SuccessFactors HCM business rules.
  • By simplifying and automating repetitive contingent workforce tasks, HR teams and managers can reduce manual effort and focus more on strategic workforce planning and operational efficiency.
  • Detailed success and failure logs provide clear visibility into processing outcomes, supporting audits, reconciliation activities, and operational reporting without additional manual intervention.

Conclusion

Managing contingent workforce data becomes increasingly complex as the numbers grow. I’ve seen how manual updates, repeated validations, and constant follow-ups can make even simple tasks time-consuming and difficult to manage.

The Contingent Data Management Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM helps bring structure and consistency into this process. Built on SAP Business Technology Platform, it enables organizations to handle large volumes of operations with better accuracy and control.

For me, this solution is not just about efficiency — it’s about reducing the operational burden on HR teams. By simplifying repetitive tasks, it allows them to focus more on planning and less on manual effort.

When the process becomes both streamlined and reliable, managing contingent workforce data feels far more manageable and scalable.

entry.title: Re: SAP System Changes Required – Transition from Income-...
entry.content: Check notes as mentioned below by SAP 1) Implement the note 3731455 (Please refer attachment “TMG Steps”) (This has provided a structure of new table FINWITH_TAXKEY for mapping new withholding tax law sections) 2) Once above is done, then implement the note 3726413 ( This will allow to keep the names of the sections more than 4 characters ) 3) The existing Official Withholding Tax Keys are not automatically mapped to the new statutory Law Sections and must be manually aligned. For this manual configuration will be done post implementation of above two notes. 4) These changes only affect the preparation of quarterly withholding tax (TDS/TCS) returns. They do not change how transactions are posted in the system and have no impact on daily accounting processes

entry.title: Re: Where can I check withholding tax history?
entry.content:
Good day @Devi_Narasimha1 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Where can I view both the old and new WHT details in one location?

Answer: You can compare/view old and new values in the Business Partner change history/change tracking area, not the normal supplier maintenance screen itself. KBA 3074615, 2808849, and 3354819 provides details on where you can view change history.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/9201dba349324ec1804d4f43ef38c8c9.html?locale=en-US

So, in practice, this means:

  • Current WHT details are visible in Manage Business Partner Master Data
  • Old and new WHT values together can be viewed through the change history/track changes function for that business partner

A quick way you can check in your system is as follows:

Open Manage Business Partner Master Data, search for the supplier, company codes, Withholding Taxes.

You will see the current active WHT details but not the historical old value comparison.

To compare old and new values, in your Maintain Business Partner, switch to Supplier Number, then choose your partner,

enter relevant date range, open change entry, review the change fields and check withholding tax related fields. This will allow you to compare what was changed from old to new.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SPP SNC (Supply Portal Collaboration) Portal - BADI - Web Dynpro Screen EPN Column inclusion
entry.content:
Introduction:

When we need to modify an existing column in SNC(Supply Network Collaboration) Portal under any tab with Header name and column values, we need to follow follow several set of procedures to find correct BADI and implement it.

In this blog I've explained about adding column EPN(Engineering Part Number) under Release Process->Overview tab in SNC portal.

Body:

Development Completed on Feb 5 , 2026
  1. Business requested to include EPN(Engineering Part Number) column in the SAP SNC(Supply Network Collaboration) Portal.
  2. T-Code for SNC Portal under APO box is /SAPAPO/SPP_CUSTOMER or we can also use /SAPAPO/SPP_SUPPLIER
  3. SNC Poral Initial Screen
  4. Under this screen go to Release Process->Overview Tab.
  5. Here you can see 7th column named as "My Product Number" should be changed to "Eng. Part Number (EPN)" column with the necessary EPN numbers to be populated against every Product number.
Arriving at Solution:
  1. After going to Release Process->Overview tab, go to debugging screen and need to find what are the available and useful BADI's for this solution to enhance this standard Web Dynpro screen.
  2. To trigger debugging WD screen within SAP system, go to T-Code /N/SCF/WD_START and provide our Application ID : /SCF/SPP_CUSTOMER and check the checkbox "Internal Modus". So that it Web Dynpro Screen will start within SAP system. We can set breakpoint at "CALL BADI" statement and find the available BADI's.
  3. Other way to find the class involved in particular Web Dynpro Screen is go to T-Code "/N/SCF/SCREENCFG" and click on ICH and go to screen definitions, then click on DSOVW-Release process Overview Screen (Supplier View) and click on Screen Layout. You will get the classes involved in fetching data etc.
  4. This process is same for all Web Dynpro screens to get the respective class name.
  5. With help of class name, we can get the logics involved in populating the screen data and BADI's called in-between.
  6. We got BADI "/SCF/RELEASE_UI" which is useful here to populate the data from the backend. Create Enhancement Implementation and one BADI implementation within the created Enhancement Implementation.
  7. BADI 1:
    BADI Definition Name/SCF/RELEASE_UI
    Interface Used/SCF/IF_EX_RELEASE_UI
    Class where BADI is Triggered /SCF/CL_MDLTBV_DSOVERVIEW
    Method called in ClassLOAD_DATA
    Enhancement ImplementationZ*
    BADI ImplementationZ*
    Implementing ClassZ*
    Method used/SCF/IF_EX_RELEASE_UI~LOAD_OVERVIEW_TBV
  8. Always keep your enhancement codes within Enhancement Switch which is maintained in a separate custom table. So that, whenever there's a misfortune happens, we can turn it off immediately to prevent more damages. Always check the Switch turned on and write your own logic.
  9. In this BADI code logic, we are fetching all the EPN values from Transparent Table /SAPAPO/MATAPN and assigning against the column "CUSTMATERIAL "(My Product Number column).
  10. This BADI will push the data into the target column.
  11. Next, we need another BADI to change the Column header name in Web Dynpro Screen.
  12. We got BADI "WD_BADI_DOMODIFYVIEW" which will be triggered multiple times. Hence, we have filter option where we need to fill required parameters.
  13. BADI 2:
    BADI Definition NameWD_BADI_DOMODIFYVIEW
    Interface UsedIF_WD_BADI_DOMODIFYVIEW
    Class where BADI is Triggered CL_WDR_DELEGATING_VIEW
    Method called in ClassDO_MODIFY_VIEW
    Enhancement ImplementationZ*
    BADI ImplementationZ*
    Implementing ClassZ*
    Method usedIF_WD_BADI_DOMODIFYVIEW~WDDOMODIFYVIEW
  14. Here we are using COMPONENT_NAME as "SALV_WD_TABLE" and VIEW_NAME as "VIEW_TABLE".{ These BADI's aren't that easy to find like I mentioned here simply, it takes lot of effort to find each and every BADI 😊}. You can also find the exact component and view name from technical help on the WD screen.
  15.  
  16. These parameters we need to find through debugging as and when our logic needs to be executed.
  17. Create EI and BI like previous BADI and add the filter values.
  18. In the BADI code logic, check for Enhancement Switch and get the exact object where our Column Header name lies within. We need to change both Header name and Caption name (visible during Hovering the column header). 
  19. We can compare ID of both elements, but it keeps on changing from system to system, but name remains consistent. Hence, We choose name of the column.
  20. To find the element name from the WD screen, right click on the column header name from WD screen and press "Technical Help". Go to "View and View Elements" tab. Now, dropdown the "View Elements and Properties" tab. Now the dropped down screen exactly represents the properties of column header where you right clicked for technical help.
  22. Catch correct exceptions in correct order where subclass exception must be caught first, and then superclass exception can be caught. CX_ROOT must be caught at last of all exceptions. This BADI will also be triggered in other screens of SNC portal also, hence, if we miss catching exception, it will result in Screen Dump.
  23. I faced lot of difficulties while finding correct component name, view name, tooltip, text of target elements. Biggest difficulty happened was to find the class name from where the data on screen is getting populated from. 
  24. Previous State/when Enhancement Switch is turned off :

  25.  

    Current State/when Enhancement Switch is turned On:

Conclusion:

This is how we change column name and related contents in SNC Portal. I've also worked on adding column "Commitment Level" column under "Receipts and Requirements"->"Due list for Purchasing Documents" tab by implementing the BADI "/SCF/ICH_POWL". Attached complete code for your reference! Please feel free to post your comments and message me for any support regarding this. Hope this helps someone! 😊

entry.title: Designing Goods Receipt Strategies in SAP S/4HANA:An End-to-End Analysis of 101 and 107/109 Movement
entry.content:
Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, Cimcorp etc. He worked on various type of project flavor like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

1.Abstract:

 

With this paper, I want to show the major process business difference between movement type 101 Vs 107/109 in SAP S4HANA. These movement types are related to goods receipt but 101 says material is available immediately at warehouse however in 107/109, material is available once the 109 movement is executed but not during 107. However, during 107, accounting documents were created immediately, which changed the ownership immediately from vendor to customer.

As per terms, it is very clear that goods receipt is done in one step in 101 however 107/109 will have two steps to complete the goods receipt. In both cases, quality inspection will happen if quality inspection is added at material level.

Mainly this presents detailed analysis from procurement point of view wherein client is having a local vendor vs foreign vendor. Moreover, if foreign vendor is very far and takes 3 months to arrive with the customer, then we cannot ask foreign vendor for 3 months. Then in this case we will use 107/109 approach for foreign vendors.

Presenting end to end process for both the scenarios, so that everyone will get the proper guidance for designing best and compliance goods receipt process in SAP.

2.0 Introduction:

 

In current manufacturing scenarios, procurement plays very crucial role for ensuring that manufacturing is running smoothly by maintaining the better inventory control. To make the manufacturing process run, some material can be procured from multiple sources like domestic vendors and foreign vendors. Sometime, if domestic vendors are not sufficient to provide the necessary quantities, then they need to procure from foreign vendors, which is known as hybrid approach. Yes, there are complexities like, we need custom clearing agency and local vendors who can provide the logistics (from port to customer warehouse) but somehow need to accept. It is not a good decision to take ownership bit early but also need to release the supplier’s invoices but there is no other option but to proceed. Also we need to make perfect cost-effective processes, so that raw material processes are not impacting much on the cost of final product.

This presents end to end process of goods receipt process in SAP S4HANA and compares 101 vs 107/109 from Inventory availability, ownership and supplier invoicing. This gives a good picture while mapping and finalizing best procurement options.

3.0 Detailed process:

Assumptions:

#1 Material master is already created and source are already finalized

#2 Split valuation are not being used, price is same for both suppliers (Local & Foreign supplier)

#3 MRP is already executed, Purchase requisition is already created.

#4 Purchase order created with reference to Purchase requisition.

Scenario1: 107 & 109(two Steps goods receipt creation)

Movement Type 107: Goods Receipt to Valuated GR Blocked Stock

Movement type 107 is used to post goods into valuated GR blocked stock. This change the ownership of the goods is transferred to the buyer, and an accounting document is generated. However, the goods are not yet physically available in warehouse. 

Movement Type 109: Goods Receipt from Valuated GR Blocked Stock

Movement type 109 is used to transfer goods from valuated GR blocked stock to unrestricted stock. This step makes the goods available for use in warehouse inventory. 

 

#1 Purchase order creation:

Created Purchase order with “Original Acceptance”, this is the important integration point, which dictate that goods receipt strategy will have 2 steps (107 & 109).

This selection in Purchase order means, goods are accepted from an ownership / accounting perspective based on the supplier/source. It is primarily related to Ownership transfer and Logistical/financial acceptance

#2 Inbound delivery creation:

Important configuration:

1.Delivery type: ELST – It should be configured and assigned number range.

 

2.Item category: ELST – It should have movement type “109”

  1. Delivery # 180000133 created with Delivery type “ELST” and Item category “ELST:

     

 

#3 Executed 107 movement:

This step initiated when support ships the material and client will do the 107 movement using MIGO/Fiori (goods receipt). It is clearly visible that accounting document is created and ownership changes from supplier to customer. And material shows as in transit, so does not exist in physical warehouse.

#4 109 movements:

Once material physically comes to warehouse then goods receive happens using SAP Fiori (Change Inbound deliveries)

#6 Put away:  

Warehouse task is created and confirmed, finally material is in warehouse bin.

#7 Purchase order history:

Scenario2: 101(one step goods receipt)

 

#1 Purchase order creation:

Purchase order is created without “Origin Accept”, so it trigger one step goods receipt process.

#2 Inbound delivery:

Inbound delivery created with document type “EL” and item category “ELN”

#3 101 Movement:

This step was executed using SAP Fiori (Change Inbound deliveries) – Accounting document created

 

#5 Put away:

Warehouse task is created and confirmed, finally material is in warehouse bin.

 

#6 Purchase order history:

4.Conclusion:

101 scenario is applicable, when delivery time is short and after goods receipt, vendor will get ​the payment as per payment terms. In this case, when vendor sends the material as soon as ​material is received at warehouse, SAP executes the goods receipt (movement type101). ​

107/109 scenario is applicable, when delivery time is significant (more than 3 months) but vendor do not ​want to wait such long for payment. In this case, when vendor confirms that respective material is ​shipped from their side then as a step goods receipt will be completed in SAP (movement type107). ​This means material is in transit and still not received in warehouse. However, based on this ​Goods receipt (first step, Vendor payment will be made. And once material is received physically at ​warehouse then goods receipt (Second Step) will be completed (movement type 109)​.

This document helps to select the best goods receipt strategies in SAP S4HANA between 101 & 107/109.This document will also help consultants to map the process in SAP, because major configuration part is added along with end user activities. This also helps to move from 101 to 107/109 easily for same material and vice versa. This document also provides the point (Origin Accept), that decides, whether 101 approach or 109/109 approach.

entry.title: Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere: The Next Level
entry.content:
Most currency conversion setups in SAP Datasphere stop at “it’s working.” But what if you could make them smarter, more flexible, and easier to maintain, without complicating your model?

In this blog, we take the standard currency conversion in SAP Datasphere and push it a step further. Building on the traditional setup (for the basics, see the following blog), we look at a common finance scenario: a user wants to view net prices either in their original transaction currency or in a specific currency they choose through an input parameter. This is typically handled using the standard currency conversion, configured with an input parameter for the target currency and set to keep unconverted values when errors occur.

However, this setup leads to an important inconsistency. Before conversion, the amount is treated as a monetary field. After conversion, SAP classifies it as a regular measure. As a result, the converted value is aggregated like any other numeric field—potentially misleading users who assume the total represents a single currency. Meanwhile, the original monetary field (in this case NetPriceAmount) behaves correctly: SAP recognizes it as multi‑currency and displays the * symbol instead of aggregating it into a single value (like done at the Standard_Currency_Conversion field).

The challenge is that SAP’s standard currency conversion doesn’t allow us to use an input parameter as the unit column (only other fields), which prevents the converted measure from being recognized as a true currency amount.

With the improved Currency Conversion, this behavior changes entirely. Instead of relying directly on the input parameter, we populate a dedicated column with the selected currency and assign that column as the Unit Column for the converted measure. By giving this measure the semantic type “Amount with Currency,” SAP now treats it as a true monetary field. The converted value then behaves just like the original monetary column (such as NetPriceAmount), ensuring consistent aggregation rules and a clearer reporting experience.

 

With this improved setup, you get consistent semantics, cleaner aggregation, and a more intuitive reporting experience. While this approach works in both graphical views and analytical models, we’ll focus on the analytical model, as it offers the fastest and most efficient implementation. More on that later in the blog.

How-To Instructions

Phase 1: Configure the Graphical View

To begin, you must establish a base fact view that handles the logic, target currency attribute and input parameter, for your target currency.

1. Create the View:

Create the view containing the fields requiring conversion.

2. Define Input Parameter(s):

Create an input parameter (e.g., TARGET_CURRENCY) to receive the user's desired currency.

Important: Assign a default value like NO_CONV. This allows the model to differentiate between a request for conversion and keeping the original currency.

3. Create a Target Currency Attribute:

Add a calculated column (e.g., TargetCurrency_c). In this column, use logic to ensure that if the input is the default NO_CONV, the column reflects the source currency; otherwise, it takes the value from the input parameter.

4. Set Semantic Type:

Change the semantic type of this new column to Currency Code to ensure the system recognizes it during aggregation.

Phase 2: Implementation in the Analytical Model

While this logic works in graphical views, using an Analytical Model is recommended for significantly better performance—completing queries in approximately 1 second compared to 6 seconds in a graphical view (according to my measurements).

1. Generate the Model:

Create a new Analytical Model based on the graphical view configured in Phase 1. Attributes and parameters will inherit automatically.

2. Add a Conversion Measure:

Under the Measures section, select the + icon and choose Conversion Measure.

 

3. Configure Settings:

Important settings:

  1. Target Currency: Reference the TargetCurrency_c attribute column created earlier (Phase 1 – Step 3).
  2. Error Handling: Set this to Fail on Error for robust data integrity.
  3. Reference Date: Ensure a date column (like SalesDocumentDate) is selected to pull the correct exchange rates.

 For more details on configuration of the currency conversion, I refer to this SAP blog on currency conversion.

Phase 3: Validation and Testing

Use the Preview function to confirm the model behaves as expected under different scenarios:

Scenario A (Default):
Leave the parameter as NO_CONV. The model should display values in their original source currencies.

Scenario B (Conversion):
Enter a specific currency code (e.g., USD). The model should apply the conversion logic and display all values in the target currency.

Conclusion

By taking this small architectural step, adding a dynamic unit column and letting the Analytical Model do the heavy lifting, you turn currency conversion in Datasphere into something far more reliable and intuitive. Your money oriented measures keep their behavior, your totals stay honest, and your users finally get a consistent experience whether they convert or not.

If you’ve tried this approach or have questions about adapting it to your own scenario, feel free to drop a comment. I’m always curious to hear how others tackle multi‑currency challenges.

entry.title: How to Rewrite REST-style Paths to OData-style Paths in SAP Integration Suite API Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working with SAP Integration Suite – API Management and I am trying to implement a routing transformation where an incoming REST-style resource path (e.g. /customer/12345) should be rewritten into an OData-style entity key format (e.g. /Customer('12345')) before the call is forwarded to the backend system.

✅ What I am trying to achieve

Incoming request:

/customer/12345

Backend should receive:

/customer('12345')

The backend system is configured through an API Provider, which already defines:

  • Hostname
  • Port
  • Base Path
  • Location ID
  • Authentication (Basic Auth)
  • On-Premise connectivity (Cloud Connector)

So ideally, I would like to reuse all these settings and only rewrite the path suffix.

✅ What I have already tried

1. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PreFlow

I attempted to set a new <Path> in the TargetEndpoint:

 
<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Set>
	    <Verb>GET</Verb>
	    <Path>/customer('12345')</Path>
	</Set>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<AssignTo createNew="true" transport="http" type="request" />
</AssignMessage>

I have also tested it on the ProxyEndpoint. I have also set the target.copy.pathsuffixto false.

Result:
The backend still receives the original API Provider base path, and the rewritten path is ignored.

2. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PostFlow

3. Setting target.url dynamically

I tested rewriting the full backend URL in JavaScript or AssignMessage to bypass the API Provider:

 
<AssignVariable>
    <Name>target.url</Name>
    <Value>https://host:port/Customer('12345')</Value>
</AssignVariable>

Result:
This technically rewrites the path, but it breaks the API Provider setup:

  • Basic Authentication is no longer applied
  • Cloud Connector / On-Premise routing does not work
  • Any certificates / trust settings from the API Provider are lost
  • I receive HTTP errors such as ServiceUnavailable or BadGateway

So this option is not suitable.

Only setting a relative path /customer('12345')ignores the suffix.

4. Rewriting request.path or proxy.pathsuffix

Based on Apigee documentation, these would be ideal, but in SAP Integration Suite:

  • request.path is read-only
  • proxy.pathsuffix is read-only
  • target.basepath is read-only
  • Only target.copy.pathsuffix is writable, but it only controls whether the suffix is appended, not what it contains

So these variables cannot be used to rewrite the path.

5. Using servicecallout.requesturi

I learned that this variable only applies to ServiceCallout policies, not to the main backend request.
Therefore it cannot be used for rewriting the TargetEndpoint URI.

✅ Where I am stuck

At this point, I have not found a reliable way to:

  • Rewrite only the resource path (REST → OData),
  • While keeping all API Provider settings intact
  • And without manually setting target.url (which overrides everything)

It seems that SAP API Management does not allow rewriting the backend path when an API Provider is used, unless the path is completely removed.

✅ My question to the community

Is there a supported way in SAP Integration Suite API Management to rewrite only the backend path (e.g. for REST → OData transformation) while still using the API Provider configuration for host, base path, authentication, and connectivity?

Specifically:

  • Where would the rewriting need to happen (ProxyEndpoint vs TargetEndpoint & Pre-Flow vs. Post-Flow)?
  • Is there a recommended approach or best practice for converting REST-style paths to OData paths in API Management?
  • Or is this transformation simply not possible when using API Providers?

Any guidance, examples, or clarification would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Migration Objects listed in Help Portal but not available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm working on a migration project for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition .

I found 9 migration objects that are documented in the official Help Portal
but do NOT appear in the actual system when I search via transaction F8638
(Migration Object Search).

These objects are:
1. Business partner (Direct Transfer)
2. MM - Purchasing info record (Direct Transfer)
3. Object classification (general template) (Direct Transfer)
4. PP - Material BOM (Direct Transfer)
5. PP - Production order (Direct Transfer)
6. PP - Production version (Direct Transfer)
7. PP - Routing (Direct Transfer)
8. PP - Work center (Direct Transfer)
9. Pricing condition (sales) (Direct Transfer)

All 9 objects are:
- Listed in Help Portal with "Migration Approach: Direct Transfer"
- Have valid Business Catalogs assigned
- Marked as "Supported" in the documentation
- But NOT visible in F8638 in our Public Edition system

For comparison, similar objects exist in the system as Staging Table versions:
- "Material BOM" (Staging Table) exists
- "Production version" (Staging Table) exists
- "Routing" (Staging Table) exists
- etc.

Questions:
1. Why are these Direct Transfer versions documented but not available?
2. Is Direct Transfer approach deprecated for Public Edition?
3. Should we ignore these 9 objects and use only the Staging Table versions?
4. Is this expected behavior or a configuration issue?

Reference:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Sale Customize Report
entry.content:
Hi @Prakhar13 ,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, there is no standard CDS view that directly links invoice (billing document) with payment details. This is because invoice data belongs to SD, while payment information is managed in FI, and the connection between them is only established during the clearing process at the accounting level.

You can achieve this requirement by building a custom CDS view using multiple released views:
I_BillingDocument / I_BillingDocumentItem → for invoice details
I_JournalEntry / I_JournalEntryItem → for accounting and payment data

Relation:

Billing Document - Accounting Document - Clearing Document (Payment)

 

Standard Apps / Reports:
There is also no single standard report or Fiori app that provides combined invoice and payment details in one place.
Standard apps such as:
Clear Incoming Payments
Manage Customer Line Items
Manage Journal Entries
can show parts of the process (especially clearing), but they do not provide a consolidated view of invoice and payment together.


Hope this clarifies.

Thanks

HM Awasthi

entry.title: Introducing myself – CPA & Independent SAP Finance Consultant | China, UK & Japan Experience
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm a CPA and independent SAP Finance consultant with over a decade of experience across China, the UK, and Japan. My career has taken me through SAP implementations in multiple markets, giving me a cross-regional perspective that I find invaluable when working on complex enterprise finance projects.

My focus areas are:

  • S/4HANA Finance (FI/CO) – intercompany processes, bank connectivity, electronic bank statement reconciliation
  • Japan-specific localisation – Zengin transfers, Japanese consumption tax, F110 payment runs
  • China-specific finance operations – cross-border intercompany flows, Chinese GAAP alignment, multi-entity structures
  • AI readiness for enterprise finance – evaluating where Joule and AI agents can deliver real value vs. where the limitations are

I'm currently deep in a Japan-based S/4HANA implementation, and before that I spent several years working on SAP projects in the UK and China.

I'm here to contribute to discussions on SAP Finance, share insights from multi-market implementations, and learn from the community.

Looking forward to connecting with fellow SAP practitioners.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QDGG App for external auditor
entry.content: Hi, The app "Display Static System Audit," designed for external auditors, offers a detailed view of these Security Audit Log events. SAP Note 2903873 lists the events currently recorded. This app enables users to access security-relevant event information within the SAP system, which can be crucial for audits. It records events such as changes to the ABAP platform, logon attempts, and transaction starts, providing transparency and helping to reconstruct sequences of events. Users can view audit analysis reports from specified log files.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG News: AI Use Cases delivered, demoed and presented
entry.content:
Really appreciate this detailed walkthrough of the AI use cases in SAP MDG. The Guided Process Creation capability within Joule  where users get guided assistance for Business Partner creation with duplicate detection directly in the chat experience  is one of those features that sounds simple but solves a real pain point. Most duplicate BP issues don't happen because users ignore the duplicate check; they happen because the check comes too late in the process, after the user has already invested time filling fields. Catching it in the chat flow changes the behavior entirely.

The Attribute Recommendation Agent concept is also worth watching  automated quality remediation that runs continuously rather than waiting for a periodic cleanse cycle is where MDG needs to go. This kind of intelligent data handling also complements broader initiatives like SAP Dart Implementation, especially for organizations focusing on audit readiness, compliance, and structured data extraction.

One question: for organizations still in classic mode, what's the recommended migration path to cloud-ready mode to unlock the full Joule integration without disrupting existing MDG workflows?

entry.title: VAT ID x country mismatch: impact on tax determination, billing output and reporting
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we are currently facing a question regarding VAT handling in Public Cloud and would appreciate your input.

In our current setup, the system checks whether the VAT ID of a customer matches the country of the business partner.
If there is a mismatch (e.g. customer based in Ireland but VAT ID from Austria), the system treats the VAT ID as invalid.

As a consequence:

  • a different tax determination is applied 
  • the customer is processed as if no valid VAT ID exists
  • the VAT ID is not printed on the billing document, even though it is maintained in the master data

From a system logic perspective, this behavior is understandable.

However, according to our customer, the business expectation is different:
The customer should still be treated as having a valid VAT ID (as one is actually maintained), regardless of the country mismatch.

This would imply:

  • a different tax determination 
  • and that the VAT ID should be printed on the billing document

Our key questions:

  • Is there a recommended way to handle such scenarios in standard?
  • How does this situation impact VAT reporting (e.g. EC Sales List or similar declarations)?
  • Does anyone know how this affects tax reporting / payment declarations in practice?

We want to ensure that both document output and legal reporting are consistent and compliant.

Any insights or experiences would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!
Katharina

entry.title: Re: Invoice delivery audit
entry.content:
Dear @eho-v , 

You may try the following options based on your business requirement,

1. Use "Shipped Goods Account for Customer"
You can configure the Shipped Goods Account for Customer. Once the delivery is created, the stock value will be parked in this account. When the customer confirms receipt of the material, you can proceed with the A/R Invoice.
If required, you can also create a customized query/report to track open delivery documents (not yet invoiced), including quantities and values. This will help you identify the stock that is still considered your asset.

2. Use Reserve Invoice
Another option is to create a Reserve Invoice first and then proceed with the Delivery. Which you've tried already..

3. Use Drop Ship Warehouse
You may also consider using a Drop Ship Warehouse during the Delivery. At the time of invoicing, you can change it to the actual warehouse. This method helps in tracking stock ownership until the final transaction is completed.

You may evaluate these options and choose the one that best suits your business process.

entry.title: Situation Handling for Journal Entry Posting – Triggering Issue with CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance Situation Handling 

Dear experts,

I am working with Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have a requirement to notify specific users via Fiori notification or email when a Journal Entry is posted, specifically based on criteria such as specific G/L accounts or Journal Entry Types.

I have completed the configuration for the following components:

  • Responsibility Management: Team and functions defined.

  • Situation Object / Scenario / Type: Configured and enabled the Situation Type.

However, despite these settings, the situation is not triggering as expected. When checking the Monitor Situations app and Application Logs, there is no evidence of any instance being created or any error logs being generated.

In my technical investigation, I am focusing on the anchor object class CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT. However, it is unclear exactly how or when this class is triggered within the Public Cloud environment.

My questions are:

  1. Triggering Mechanism: Does the ABAP event class CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT trigger the situation in real-time immediately upon Journal Entry posting (Save event)? Or does it require a background job scheduled via the "Schedule Situation Handling Jobs" app?

  2. Standard Workaround or Recommended Approach: Is there a specific standard Situation Scenario or Job Template recommended for triggering notifications based on Journal Entry attributes in Public Cloud?

  3. Known Limitation by Design: Is the direct triggering of situations from the Journal Entry posting event a known limitation in the current version of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

The goal is to inform relevant stakeholders immediately when a specific posting occurs using the standard Situation Handling framework, avoiding custom BAdI developments where possible.

Any guidance, best practices, or confirmation regarding the behavior of this specific class and situation object would be greatly appreciated.

Regards,
M. Kitakatsu

entry.title: Struggling to automate SAP WebGUI testing.
entry.content:
Struggling with automating SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) using OPA5 — wondering if others have faced the same.

While OPA5 works well for UI5 apps, things start breaking down when WebGUI comes into the picture. Since WebGUI runs inside an iframe and isn’t a UI5 application, OPA5 doesn’t seem to recognize or interact with those elements at all.

Some of the issues I’ve been hitting:

• Unable to locate or interact with WebGUI elements using OPA5
• Controls exist in DOM but OPA5 cannot access them
• Dynamic IDs and rendering make selectors unreliable
• Switching context to iframe isn’t supported in OPA5 the way it’s needed
• Hybrid apps (UI5 + WebGUI) create a major gap in automation strategy

At this point, I’m trying to figure out if there’s a reliable way to handle WebGUI automation within an OPA5-based setup — or if a completely different approach is needed.

Would love to hear if anyone has tackled this problem or found a workaround.

#SAP #OPA5 #WebGUI #TestAutomation #UI5

entry.title: Supplier rebate / allowance scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to understand the best-practice way to handle a supplier rebate / allowance scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Our case is not only supplier-based, but also product-structure based. For example, a product may belong to a hierarchy like: Machines → Electronics → Hovers → Phonex. An agreement may apply at different levels, such as a specific product, a subgroup like Hovers, or a higher group like Electronics.

Also, we may buy the same product from different suppliers, and each supplier may have different conditions for that same product or hierarchy level. For example:

  • Supplier A gives 10% rebate for Hovers if purchase quantity or value reaches a certain level
  • Supplier B has a different rule for the same Hovers
  • A supplier may also give a fixed monthly amount for display/shelf space
  • Some suppliers or products may have no special agreement

From what I understand, the standard area in SAP for this is Settlement Management using supplier condition contracts, including settlement of supplier rebates and fixed-amount agreements.

My questions are:

  1. Is this the correct standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. Can the agreement logic be maintained by supplier + product / product group / hierarchy level?
  3. For fixed monthly amounts like display support, is it usually handled as part of the same supplier settlement logic, or as a separate process?
  4. If part of the agreement depends on our sales to customers instead of only purchasing-related data, is that supported in standard Public Cloud?

My goal is to make sure we can clearly track:

  • what we pay each supplier
  • what we should receive back as rebate/credit
  • and how this is settled in SAP

I would appreciate guidance from anyone who implemented a similar scenario in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Is It Possible to Split PST File by Size or Date?
entry.content:
You can split a PST file by size or date, and you can start with a free manual method.
You can use Microsoft Outlook's built-in Archive feature to split the PST file by date. In Outlook, click on File, then Info, then Tools, and finally Clean Up Old Items (Archive). Select the folder you want to split and a specific date range after that. Outlook will make a new PST file with older emails in it. This will split your data by date. This method is free and works well for simple tasks, but it can't split files by size and can be slow for big files.
You can use a professional PST Converter if the manual method doesn't work for you or if you need more advanced options like splitting by size, folder, or bulk data. It's easy to split PST files with a trusted tool like BLR Tools. It keeps emails, attachments, and structure intact so that you won't lose any data.

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: What's New in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
entry.content:
Hello @MatthiasL, 
 
There are no changes of this new SAP GRC for SAP HANA version on SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance. 
 
As you may already know, while SAP provides dedicated SaaS applications such as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance and SAP Risk and Assurance Management for instance, for customers preferring a private cloud or on-premise deployment it was time for SAP to reinvent GRC software to accompany customers for their challenges of the next decades. Hence this new version of SAP GRC solutions on-premises or in the private cloud.
 
With regards to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, it can of course still connect to SAP Access Control via the IAG Bridge scenario that facilitates the creation of access requests and performs risk analysis for cloud applications.
In case this is of interest, you will be able to find more details on this integration scenario at the following location: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE/5c19369f0b924946abc80242ffaa2f27/b75c7a2c00144c2abcebc482b719605b.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US
 
There is also a dedicated GRC Tuesdays blog on this scenario: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/grc-tuesdays-iag-bridge-to-the-cloud/ba-p/12828124
 
Kind regards, 
Thomas
